title: Tried a DeFi AI trading bot. Early thoughts
author: emlanis
selftext:
I’ve been testing a new AI trading bot called Nolly on testnet and thought I’d share a quick experience here since it sits somewhere between AI agents and onchain trading.

Setup was straightforward. It generated a wallet for me, and the flow is command-based. You fund it, switch to auto mode, optionally define instructions, and then trigger execution. It feels more like interacting with an agent than using a typical trading UI.

What stood out to me is the structure:

\- It separates gas, collateral, and AI credits clearly

\- You can define how the AI should behave instead of just pressing buy or sell

\- It runs on top of a leverage system rather than spot trading

So the idea is an execution through a managed position, not just signals.

Right now it’s still testnet, so I’m not judging performance yet. I’m more interested in how this model plays out long term.

If AI trading keeps moving in this direction, then the interface changes completely. Instead of dashboards and charts, you’re basically giving instructions to an agent that handles positioning for you.

A few things I’m still watching:

\- How it handles risk during volatility

\- Whether instructions actually influence behavior meaningfully

\- How transparent the decision process becomes over time

Curious if anyone else here is testing similar agent-based trading setups. Feels like we’re moving away from manual trading faster than expected.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
Hey there, I have my own profitable ML-based trading model for BTC (and it also works on Gold). I’ve tested it on about 5 years of data and it averaged roughly around 10% per month with fixed lot. It has a sharpe ratio of >3. I don’t have much capital myself, so I’m looking for genuine people who might be interested to buying it. If anyone’s genuinely interested, feel free to message me.

This is my report - [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive_link)

Also to say I am live trading it since September and inspite of Spot Drawdown my model has actually generated positive returns.

comments.author: FilmFreak1082
comments.body:
Interesting concept but the "give instructions to an agent" model has a fundamental problem: you dont know if the agent understood your instructions correctly until it loses money.

"Define how the AI should behave" sounds great in theory. In practice - did it interpret "be conservative during high volatility" the same way you meant it? What counts as "high"? When does "conservative" become "do nothing"? Every ambiguous word is a potential misexecution.

I build trading bots and went the opposite direction deliberately. No natural language instructions. No AI interpreting intent. The algorithm is pure math - if price dips X% with Y momentum and Z volume, buy this amount. Same input always gives same output. Boring but I can audit every single decision and know exactly why it happened.

2,300+ trades last month, 92.7% win rate on spot. No leverage. When a trade goes wrong I open the log and see the exact numbers that triggered entry. Try doing that with "I told the agent to be moderately aggressive on high-cap tokens during accumulation phases."

The things youre watching for - risk during volatility, whether instructions influence behavior, transparency of decisions - those are the right questions. But theyre also the questions that should already have answers BEFORE you fund the thing, not after.

Were definitely moving away from manual trading. But I think the future is transparent automation, not opaque AI agents. The trust gap is already huge in crypto. Adding a layer of "the AI decided" doesnt help close it.

replies.author: Ok_Permission3367
replies.body:
Wont work. I tried the pure math approach and it fails due to the human factor. Some trades were perfect on math but were blind to shifting market sentiment from the news or didn't see in the background SEC actions etc. 

Soooo... I went pure math then added an autonomous AI review layer before execution that gate keeps baaed on all the human stuff. Currently in test. Backtest shows promise.
